turns.user_message: A person I know just casually mentioned I should probably think before I speak, and it completely ruined my whole afternoon. I keep replaying the exact moment they said it, wondering what I did wrong to make them look at me like that. It feels like I’m always accidentally stepping on people’s toes without even realizing it. I just want to rewind the conversation and fix whatever tone I used, but I know it’s too late now. I’m left sitting here feeling completely hollow and embarrassed over a few simple words.

That offhand remark keeps echoing in my head every time I try to join a group chat or reply to a message. I find myself typing out responses and deleting them over and over because I’m terrified of sounding careless again. It’s turning into this constant habit where I just assume everyone is quietly judging my every move. I hate how quickly I went from feeling normal to feeling like I’m a burden in every conversation. The guilt just piles up until I stop talking altogether to avoid making another mistake.

I can’t stop dissecting every single interaction I’ve had lately, convinced that I’m permanently marked as someone who ruins the mood. That one comment has completely shattered my confidence, making me feel like I’m fundamentally flawed at the core. I keep imagining how much easier it would be for everyone else if I just stayed completely out of the way. The shame is so heavy that I feel physically weighed down by my own presence. I’m just exhausted from carrying this constant fear that I’m disappointing people I haven’t even properly met yet.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This just started recently.
